name: ai-adopters
description: Mid-to-large non-AI-native companies showing AI-adoption signals - the ICP for AI consulting, training, and enablement vendors

hiringLabels.description: a role that owns AI adoption, enablement, transformation, or strategy across the organization (e.g. Head of AI, AI Program Manager, AI Enablement Lead) - not a hands-on engineering role
a hands-on AI/ML engineering, data science, or applied research role building AI systems
a data engineering, data platform, or analytics infrastructure role

weights.hypothesis: A named AI-adoption owner means budget and a mandate exist but the org's skills don't yet - peak receptivity for outside training and enablement help
In-house AI engineering hiring at a non-AI-native company signals committed budget - the builders will need the rest of the org brought along
Data-platform investment is the groundwork phase of AI adoption - earlier in the journey, weaker but real intent
Generic engineering volume shows organizational health, not AI intent
A recent raise at a mid-market company funds transformation initiatives - AI programs are where boards push that spend now
Public AI announcements commit leadership to outcomes ahead of internal capability - narrative precedes skills

compounds.hypothesis: A public AI narrative plus a freshly-hired internal owner is the strongest why-now - they have promised something they cannot yet deliver
